title: Boil water alert: supply restrictions for Darwin amid ‘unprecedented’ dam flood | ABC NEWS
description: More than a hundred thousand residents of the Northern Territory's Top End are tonight being told to boil tap water for drinking, cooking, making baby formula and brushing teeth, after major flooding compromised Darwin's main drinking water supply.  Locals also told to ration water usage as engineers work frantically to restore operations. Courtney Barrett-Peters reports for ABC News Northern Territory. title: Apocalyptic scenes in Iran after Israel's strike on major oil depots in Tehran | ABC NEWS
description: An Iranian man has told ABC News "black rain is falling from the sky" in Tehran in the wake of Israeli attacks on oil depots.  The Iranian Red Crescent is warning residents to stay indoors and wear masks if possible due to the thick black smoke blanketing the capital. It's also urging residents to avoid contact with any further rain that may fall in the city, citing the risk of skin burns and lung damage from acidic rain. title: River swells in Daly, heavy rainfall threatens Katherine flood recovery | ABC NEWS
description: 00:00 Communities in the Katherine region are still dealing with a major flood emergency.   The Katherine River is slowly falling, tonight, sitting at 15.99 metres after inundating homes and businesses at its peak of 19.2 metres on Saturday.   In nearby Beswick, the community hasn't been evacuated, and the Waterhouse River is currently at 8.43 metres.  Focus is now turning to the Daly River, which is still rising, at 14.97 metres, The highest level in decades.   Our reporter James Elton has this update for ABC News Northern Territory.   02:20 Floods update and relief payments interview with Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro. title: Iranian-Australians try to communicate with footballers at Sydney Airport | The World | ABC NEWS
description: There have been tensions between police and members of the Iranian community at Sydney airport as some supporters tried to pass messages on to members of Iran's women's football team, urging them to seek asylum. Members of the team arrived in Sydney and were transferred by bus to the international terminal, ahead of a flight leaving the country.   The ABC understands at least seven members of the team are now seeking asylum in Australia.
